parent: OOF accuracy must not decrease and ensemble epistemic error-detection AUC should exceed the baseline.
current: OOF accuracy or balanced accuracy must improve without degrading a majority of folds.

Five independently initialized patient-level MIL models improve accuracy and epistemic error detection.
current: Fold-local self-supervised spectral pretraining improves representations and corrects systematic high-confidence errors.

parent: exp_001_ensemble
exp_000_baseline
Mean predictive probabilities approximate Bayesian model averaging.
Sixteen spectra preserve patient heterogeneity while fitting grouped training in GPU memory.

current: Kept secondary so reconstruction remains the dominant spectroscopy objective.
Retains global spectral shape while masked loss emphasizes missing intervals.

parent: Multi-scale receptive fields cover narrow Raman peaks and broader spectral patterns.
Prevent the heteroscedastic head from escaping hard examples through unlimited variance.

current: Forces the convolutional backbone to model local Raman peak structure rather than labels.

parent: Five is the smallest conventional deep ensemble with useful between-model variance.

current: Encourages stability to physically plausible noise, masking, and small wavenumber shifts.
All five members in one fold start from the same leakage-safe encoder; supervised randomness still differs.
A separate encoder is pretrained using only the training patients of each OOF fold.

parent: Separates member random streams while preserving identical patient folds.

current: Equal patient contribution prevents acquisition count from becoming an SSL shortcut.

parent: Fitted only from cross-fitted OOF development predictions.
The 11-patient holdout is already observed and cannot support a new confirmatory claim.
current: The 11-patient holdout remains historical and is not used for pretraining or selection.

parent: Optimized only from calibrated OOF development predictions.
Four bags are jointly optimized as one patient-level training item.
Dynamic resampling gives equal optimization weight to every patient.
Stabilizes class separation before optimizing aleatoric variance.
